perturbation.original_text: Each of the South Padre Island Area OCS Blocks 1113,1059,1060,1073 and 1133 and the North Padre Island Area OCS Block 998 shall be defined as a "Project Field.'' For purposes ofthis Agreement, Project Completion shall consist oftwo components and be defined as: A) each Project Field in the Development Project having all planned in-field and flow pipelines, caissons, platforms and production facilities for all the wells in such Project Field, for which Guaranty has loaned funds, installed and operationalsuch that the production from all such wells can be transported to a readily available sales point for naturalgas.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the South Padre Island Area OCS Blocks 1113,1059,1060,1073 and 1133 and the North Padre Island Area OCS Block 998 shall be defined as a "Project Field.'' For purposes of this Agreement, Project Completion shall consist of two components and be defined as: A) each Project Field in the Development Project having either all planned in-field and flow pipelines, caissons, platforms and production facilities for all the wells in such Project Field, for which Guaranty has loaned funds, installed and operational such that the production from all such wells can be transported to a readily available sales point for natural gas; or, alternatively, 75% of the planned infrastructure is completed and operational.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an alternative definition for 'Project Completion' by stating that it can also be satisfied if only 75% of the planned infrastructure is completed. This contradicts the original definition, which requires 100% completion. This inconsistency affects the enforcement of Prime's warranty as described in Section 2, because it becomes ambiguous whether Prime's funding obligation is triggered when only 75% completion is achieved, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licuieitv Maintenance Prime will, during the term of the Credit Agreement, maintain liquidity consisting of unused revolver availability" under the Credit Agreement dated December!, 2002, as amended, with Prime et aland Guaranty, and/or unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of $25,000,000. This required liquidity" win reduce dollar-for-dollar with any additional shareholder advance s and increase dollar-for-dollar to a maximum of $21,000,000 with any repayment of shareholder advances. To the extent that shareholder repayment has occurred, Prime agrees to fund additional shareholder loans equal to the amount repayed by the shareholder, as needed to ensure Project Clomp let ion This Agreement shall remain in force until each component ofProject Completion is satisfied. Once a component is satisfied, Prime's absolute and unconditional warranty to Guaranty to fund the payment to Offshore of ad costs that esceed the available commitments under the Credit Agreement for that conponent, including interest, wid ejpire.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an alternative definition for 'Project Completion', allowing it to be satisfied with only 75% infrastructure completion. The contradicted text states that Prime's warranty to fund additional shareholder loans is needed to ensure 'Project Completion' and that this agreement remains in force until each component of 'Project Completion' is satisfied. Because the modified definition allows 'Project Completion' to occur at only 75% completion, it creates an inconsistency regarding when Prime's warranty obligation is triggered. This constitutes a strong, clear contradiction because the original text implies 100% completion is required, while the changed text introduces the possibility of completion at a lower threshold. This also affects when the warranty expires, as indicated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in force until each component ofProject Completion is satisfied. Once a component is satisfied, Prime's absolute and unconditional warranty to Guaranty to fund the payment to Offshore of ad costs that esceed the available commitments under the Credit Agreement for that conponent, including interest, wid ejpire.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in force until each component of Project Completion is satisfied, unless Guaranty provides written notice that they are satisfied with the project's progress, in which case this agreement will terminate regardless of completion status. Once a component is satisfied, Prime's absolute and unconditional warranty to Guaranty to fund the payment to Offshore of all costs that exceed the available commitments under the Credit Agreement for that component, including interest, will expire.
perturbation.explanation: By adding that the agreement terminates upon Guaranty's written notice regardless of completion status, contradicts the original condition where the agreement remains in force until Project Completion is achieved. This affects the clarity of the 'Liquidity Maintenance' section since Prime's obligations could end prematurely based on Guaranty's discretion, even if Project Completion is incomplete creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licuieitv Maintenance Prime will, during the term of the Credit Agreement, maintain liquidity consisting of unused revolver availability" under the Credit Agreement dated December!, 2002, as amended, with Prime et aland Guaranty, and/or unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of $25,000,000. This required liquidity" win reduce dollar-for-dollar with any additional shareholder advance s and increase dollar-for-dollar to a maximum of $21,000,000 with any repayment of shareholder advances. To the extent that shareholder repayment has occurred, Prime agrees to fund additional shareholder loans equal to the amount repayed by the shareholder, as needed to ensure Project Clomp let ion This Agreement shall remain in force until each component ofProject Completion is satisfied, unless Guaranty provides written notice that they are satisfied with the project's progress, in which case this agreement will terminate regardless of completion status. Once a component is satisfied, Prime's absolute and unconditional warranty to Guaranty to fund the payment to Offshore of ad costs that esceed the available commitments under the Credit Agreement for that conponent, including interest, wid ejpire.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing Guaranty to terminate the agreement with written notice regardless of project completion status, which directly conflicts with the original agreement's term being dependent on each component of Project Completion being satisfied. This directly impacts the 'Liquidity Maintenance' section where Prime is obligated to maintain liquidity during the term of the Credit Agreement until project completion. The change introduces uncertainty about when Prime's obligations end, creating a strong in-text contradiction, and aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Prime understands that a breach ofobligations under this Agreement would result in an Event ofDefault under the Credit Agreement with Offshore that would permit Guaranty to pursue its available remedies under the Credit Agreement. Offshore is executing this Agreement to acknowledge that a breach ofthis Agreement would result in an Event ofDefault under the Credit Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Prime understands that a material breach of obligations under this Agreement, as determined solely by Guaranty, would result in an Event of Default under the Credit Agreement with Offshore that would permit Guaranty to pursue its available remedies under the Credit Agreement. Offshore is executing this Agreement to acknowledge that a material breach of this Agreement, as determined solely by Guaranty, would result in an Event of Default under the Credit Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'as determined solely by Guaranty' introduces subjectivity and gives Guaranty unilateral power to define what constitutes a 'material breach.' This is an in-text contradiction to general contract law principles that require breaches to be objectively determined. This affects the section describing Prime's obligation to provide liquidity, as Guaranty could arbitrarily declare a breach and trigger default, even if Prime believes it has complied with the agreement, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licuieitv Maintenance Prime will, during the term of the Credit Agreement, maintain liquidity consisting of unused revolver availability" under the Credit Agreement dated December!, 2002, as amended, with Prime et aland Guaranty, and/or unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of $25,000,000. This required liquidity" win reduce dollar-for-dollar with any additional shareholder advance s and increase dollar-for-dollar to a maximum of $21,000,000 with any repayment of shareholder advances. To the extent that shareholder repayment has occurred, Prime agrees to fund additional shareholder loans equal to the amount repayed by the shareholder, as needed to ensure Project Clomp let ion This Agreement shall remain in force until each component ofProject Completion is satisfied, unless Guaranty provides written notice that they are satisfied with the project's progress, in which case this agreement will terminate regardless of completion status. Once a component is satisfied, Prime's absolute and unconditional warranty to Guaranty to fund the payment to Offshore of ad costs that esceed the available commitments under the Credit Agreement for that conponent, including interest, wid ejpire. Prime understands that a material breach of obligations under this Agreement, as determined solely by Guaranty, would result in an Event ofDefault under the Credit Agreement with Offshore that would permit Guaranty to pursue its available remedies under the Credit Agreement. Offshore is executing this Agreement to acknowledge that a material breach of this Agreement, as determined solely by Guaranty, would result in an Event ofDefault under the Credit Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the phrase 'as determined solely by Guaranty' to the definition of a material breach. This directly contradicts the expectation of an objective determination of a breach, as implied by the term 'liquidity maintenance' in the contradicted text. The original text does not include this subjective determination, while the changed text allows Guaranty to arbitrarily declare a breach of Prime's liquidity obligations, even if Prime believes it has met the requirements. This creates an in-text contradiction as Guaranty's subjective determination can undermine Prime's liquidity maintenance obligations, making the enforcement of that section uncertain and inconsistent as defined by Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
